claim: WHO Global TB Report 2024 estimates 10.6 million incident TB cases, 1.3 million deaths, 4 million undiagnosed or unreported cases, with India, Indonesia, China, Philippines, and Pakistan carrying 60 percent of global burden.
method: Reviewed WHO TB report chapters on burden, detection gap, treatment outcomes, and country profiles.
limitations: Burden estimates are modeled and depend on prevalence survey inputs of variable recency.
Sub-national estimates are not systematically reported.

claim: A 2022 analysis of India's TB notification data found private-sector notifications increased from 19 percent to 32 percent after mandatory notification policy, but 40 percent of initial care-seeking still occurred in the private sector, much of it unreported.

source.title: Private sector engagement in tuberculosis care in India: progress and gaps

method: Reviewed private-sector notification trends, mandatory-notification impact, and remaining gaps.
limitations: Notification data completeness varies by state and private-provider type.
Patients may seek care from multiple providers, complicating notification deduplication.
